title: senecio bracteolatus var. bracteolatus
intro: Senecio bracteolatus var. bracteolatus is a plant species commonly known as the white iron bark or fern-leaved polyantha, and is native to eastern Australia. This plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Senecio bracteolatus var. bracteolatus include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and can be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
